ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Prophet frowned and turned away
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He frowned and turned ˹his attention˺ away,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Because there came to him the blind man, [interrupting].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹simply˺ because the blind man came to him ˹interrupting˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But what would make you perceive, [O Muhammad], that perhaps he might be purified
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You never know ˹O Prophet˺, perhaps he may be purified,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or be reminded and the remembrance would benefit him?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: or he may be mindful, benefitting from the reminder.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As for he who thinks himself without need,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for the one who was indifferent,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To him you give attention.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: you gave him your ˹undivided˺ attention,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And not upon you [is any blame] if he will not be purified.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: even though you are not to blame if he would not be purified.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But as for he who came to you striving [for knowledge]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But as for the one who came to you, eager ˹to learn˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: While he fears [Allah],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: being in awe ˹of Allah˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From him you are distracted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: you were inattentive to him.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, these verses are a reminder;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! This ˹revelation˺ is truly a reminder.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So whoever wills may remember it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So let whoever wills be mindful of it.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is recorded] in honored sheets,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is ˹written˺ on pages held in honour—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Exalted and purified,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: highly esteemed, purified—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Carried] by the hands of messenger-angels,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: by the hands of angel-scribes,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Noble and dutiful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: honourable and virtuous.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Cursed is man; how disbelieving is he.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Condemned are ˹disbelieving˺ humans! How ungrateful they are ˹to Allah˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From what substance did He create him?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: From what substance did He create them?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From a sperm-drop He created him and destined for him;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He created them from a sperm-drop, and ordained their development.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then He eased the way for him;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then He makes the way easy for them,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then He causes his death and provides a grave for him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then causes them to die and be buried.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then when He wills, He will resurrect him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then when He wills, He will resurrect them.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Man has not yet accomplished what He commanded him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! They have failed to comply with what He ordered.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then let mankind look at his food -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Let people then consider their food:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How We poured down water in torrents,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: how We pour down rain in abundance

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We broke open the earth, splitting [it with sprouts],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and meticulously split the earth open ˹for sprouts˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And caused to grow within it grain
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: causing grain to grow in it,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And grapes and herbage
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as grapes and greens,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And olive and palm trees
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and olives and palm trees,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And gardens of dense shrubbery
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and dense orchards,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fruit and grass -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and fruit and fodder—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [As] enjoyment for you and your grazing livestock.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: all as ˹a means of˺ sustenance for you and your animals.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when there comes the Deafening Blast
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then, when the Deafening Blast comes to pass—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On the Day a man will flee from his brother
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: on that Day every person will flee from their own siblings,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his mother and his father
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹even˺ their mother and father,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his wife and his children,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹even˺ their spouse and children.

ayahs.textArabic: لِكُلِّ ٱمۡرِيٕٖ مِّنۡهُمۡ يَوۡمَئِذٖ شَأۡنٞ يُغۡنِيهِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Likulli imriin minhum yawmaithin shanun yughneehi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For every man, that Day, will be a matter adequate for him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For then everyone will have enough concern of their own.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Some] faces, that Day, will be bright -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day ˹some˺ faces will be bright,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Laughing, rejoicing at good news.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: laughing and rejoicing,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [other] faces, that Day, will have upon them dust.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: while ˹other˺ faces will be dusty,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blackness will cover them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: cast in gloom—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those are the disbelievers, the wicked ones.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those are the disbelievers, the ˹wicked˺ sinners.
